title: [VR Hot] Verify IMH VR Hot Actions
description:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)

Flow *

  • This chapter is intro only. The exact spec for iMH is in Socket Thermal/DMR SOC Thermal.
  • PrimeCode periodically polls SVID_VR_STATUS register over PMSB (and not relying on SVID to notify through PM_EVENT).
  • Action would be to reduce iMH mesh frequency to P1 and send CBB dies frequency limit through HPM message per relevant SVID alert event.
  • MSR POWER_CTL[VR_THERM_ALERT_DISABLE] can disable PrimeCode action.

  • Logs this in MSR_CORE_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[PLATFORM] and MSR_RING_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[PLATFORM] (and not [PROCHOT_STATUS])

  • Detailed iMH Flow in DMR D2D Perimeter HAS



  • VR Hot Detection: Both the IMH Primecode poll their respective MBVR stack via SVID_VR_STATUS register for any SVID ALERTs. This occurs periodically in the Slowloop.
  • When the temperature exceeds the TEMP_MAX associated with the VR the MBVR asserts VRHot by setting the SVID Therm Alert bit in the SVID_STATUS register_
  • Once the IMH Primecode detects the VRHot condition it sends HPM message DNS_EVENT_DELIVERY, with VR_THERM_ALERT bit set to its leaf CBB dies and the other IMH die.
  • Note: VRHot actions are taken across all the dies in the socket regardless of which VR asserts the Therm Alert.
  • IMH Die Action: IMH Primecode reduces its fabric frequencies to P1.
  • IMH Primecode sets the RING_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[VR_THERM_ALERT_STATUS]

When the temperature cools down the MBVR clears the SVID STATUS register bit for VR Hot.

  • VR Hot de-assertion The IMH Primecode detects the VR Hot status change and sends the HPM message DNS_EVENT_DELIVERY, with VR_THERM_ALERT bit cleared to its leaf CBB dies and the other IMH die.
  • Both the IMH Primecode removes their respective Fabric frequency ceiling due to VR Hot condition and clears the RING_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[VR_THERM_ALERT_STATUS]


1. Set SVID_VR_STATUS[ThermAlert] = 1

2. Verify IMH Primecode reduces its fabric frequencies to P1

3. Verify RING_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[VR_THERM_ALERT_STATUS] is set

4. Verify SVID STATUS register bit for VR Hot is cleared

5. Verify IMH Primecode removes their respective Fabric frequency ceiling

6. Verify RING_PERF_LIMIT_REASONS[VR_THERM_ALERT_STATUS] is cleared


Configuration *

How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Examples: register values to look for, what content output to look for on the screen, key words or sections in the log.

Intent Verification Strategy *

How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e.g. on N-1 hardware, in Simics, etc.)? If not possible, explain why (e.g. new feature not present in N-1 and not modelled in pre-si environment).

HW Requirements

What besides silicon is required for execution of this test? For example: logic analyzer, a thermal head, an Intel test card or IHV device, etc.

SW Requirements

What software besides actual test content is needed for execution of this test? For example: special version of operating system, drivers, firmware, or harassers (to run with the main test content), software tool(s).

Coverage

Specify any expected coverage that is dependent on environment or configuration (not covered in the TCD's description). Also specify any coverage assessment/measurement technique that might be used, if applicable and if not stated in the TCD.

Component Interactions

Describe or illustrate the components that are involved and how they interact. This section is only needed if it provides more information than the TCD article, perhaps specific to the validation environment and framework used for this test_case.
